description: Registry of stable Vibra linter and compiler codes (reference for tooling)

codes.summary: YAML construct outside strict subset (anchors, merge, tags, binary, etc.)
Block comments forbidden in v1
Ambiguous unquoted scalar (bool/null/number) where string required
Invalid $-callee name (e.g. punctuation-only like $+); use identifier forms such as $add
$function must use canonical labeled shorthand; implicit subject, nested envelopes, and $void plus args are forbidden
$let must bind exactly one name per invocation
$function.do must be a block sequence, not nested $do wrapper
Use $do for multi-step sequencing outside $function.do
$if requires keys is, then, else
String interpolation not allowed except blessed stdlib forms
Structured `$match: { target, arms }` form is not canonical; use `$match: <expr>` with sibling `when:` arms
Import cycle detected
A module references an import alias that it does not declare directly
Variance / subtyping audit marker
Symbol-like key is not kebab-case
$wasm encoding does not match active compiler WASM mode
Unknown annotation key on a top-level definition (recognised `=`-prefixed annotations: `=doc`, `=where`; further additions documented per phase)
Legacy un-prefixed annotation key (`where:`, `doc:`); v1 annotations must use the `=` prefix (rename to `=where`, `=doc`)
`=where` bound list element does not resolve to an interface (or `$intersect` of interfaces). Bounds may be a registered `$interface` alias, an inline `$interface`, or an `$intersect` of those.

Type argument supplied to a generic call site or a generic type-position instantiation does not satisfy its declared `=where` bound. Satisfaction requires a nominal `=impl` block on the implementing type for each interface in the bound. Also raised by interface-qualified dispatch (`$iface.method`) when the dispatch argument's type has no matching `=impl`.

Interface-qualified call (`$iface.method`) targets an interface method that has no `$self`-typed argument, so dispatch is impossible. Use the type-qualified form `$<implementing-type>.<iface>.<method>` instead.

Interface-qualified call's `$self` argument has a generic static type. Dispatch through generics requires monomorphisation, which is not yet implemented in v1; pass a concrete value or call the type-qualified form.
`=doc` annotation must be a string scalar (YAML `|` block or quoted string)
Bare reference to a generic type alias requires explicit instantiation `{ $alias: { tparam: T, ... } }`
Generic alias instantiation is malformed (unknown alias, missing param, unknown param name, or arity mismatch)
Implicit coercion between a `$newtype` and its inner type is forbidden; use `$cast`
Malformed `$newtype` definition body
`$cast` has no valid v1 cast path between source and target types
Malformed `$cast` payload; expected `$cast: <expr>` with sibling `into: <type>`
Capability types are runtime-minted authority values and cannot be created with `$cast` or literals
Reserved `$self` type used outside an `$interface` body or a type's `=defs` / `=impl` annotation
Invalid `=defs` annotation (placed on a non-type definition, entry is not a `$function`, or duplicate inherent op name)
Invalid `=impl` annotation (placed on a non-type definition, malformed payload, or method binding that is neither a `$function` envelope nor a `$ref` string)
`=impl` keyed by an alias that does not resolve to a registered `$interface` type
`=impl` block missing a binding for one of the interface's `=where` type-parameters or one of the interface's methods
`=impl` payload contains an unexpected key (not `=where`, an iface type-arg, or an iface method name)
`=impl` method signature does not match the interface declaration (after `$self` and iface type-arg substitution)
`=impl` method binding is a `$ref` string that does not resolve to a registered function
Noncanonical option representation; use the tagged stdlib option enum instead of `$option` sugar or a direct `$void` union member
